domain_presentation_time_series_desc: Per-source time series with uncertainty bands.
domain_presentation_distribution_desc: Histogram + quantile summary across the scope.
domain_presentation_metric_scatter_desc: Per-article scatter — position, size, and colour each bound to a metric.
domain_presentation_topic_distribution_d: Per-language BERTopic ridgeline — what is being talked about, by volume.
domain_presentation_topic_evolution_desc: Stream graph of topic volume over time — how the expressible shifts.
domain_presentation_revision_activity_de: Silent-edit activity per source for the current window (Wayback CDX + sitemap-lastmod). Synchronic snapshot.
domain_presentation_revision_timeline_de: Silent-edit activity over time — how often each source revises, by daily / weekly / monthly bucket.
domain_presentation_revision_discourse_s: How silent edits move the discourse — mean sentiment delta and semantic (topic) shift per source over time.
domain_presentation_revision_edit_cluste: Coordinated cross-source silent edits — which entities ≥2 sources quietly changed in the same time bucket.
domain_presentation_cross_probe_lead_lag: Cross-probe temporal lead-lag — does one culture lead the other in publication rhythm?
domain_presentation_categorical_distribu: Article count per category of a metadata field (section, author, tags, …) — what kinds of articles the scope is made of.
domain_presentation_correlation_matrix_d: Pairwise Pearson correlation across a chosen set of metrics — which move together, which are independent.
domain_presentation_cross_tab_desc: A metric broken down by a metadata category — e.g. mean sentiment per section. Bars ranked by article count, coloured by the metric.
domain_presentation_metric_lead_lag_desc: Lagged cross-correlation of two metrics over time — does one consistently lead the other?
domain_presentation_parallel_coordinates: One line per article across several metric axes — read clusters and crossing patterns across many dimensions at once.
domain_presentation_sankey_desc: Article flows between categories across an ordered chain of metadata fields — where the corpus splits and merges.
domain_pillar_aleph_desc: Every observed probe in scope, no time axis beyond the active window. Snapshot-oriented analyses: sentiment levels, lexical density, distributional shape, paired-metric structure, what is being talked about right now (by volume), and which sources are currently editing most (silent-edit activity).
domain_pillar_episteme_desc: How the expressible shifts over time. Time is the axis: metric time-series, topic evolution, drift, and silent-edit activity over time — the long-term shape of what can be said within the discursive formation.
domain_pillar_rhizome_desc: How frames move. Entity co-occurrence, lead-lag, cross-probe diffusion — the relational substrate of the discourse.
domain_function_epistemic_authority_desc: Sources that produce and legitimate knowledge claims (WP-001 §3).
domain_function_power_legitimation_desc: Sources that frame, justify, or contest political power (WP-001 §3).
domain_function_cohesion_identity_desc: Sources that articulate collective identity and social cohesion (WP-001 §3).
domain_function_subversion_friction_desc: Sources that challenge dominant frames or introduce friction (WP-001 §3).
domain_ns_structural_metadata_absence_de: The publisher does not emit this metadata field — absent by design, a publisher choice (WP-003 §3.2).
domain_ns_temporal_provenance_absence_de: The timestamp is the crawler fetch time, not a real publication date — an observation gap, not a discourse gap (WP-005 §3.1).
domain_ns_silent_edit_desc: The article was edited after publication (headline change / republication / archive gap) — the visible text may not be the original (WP-003 §5.3.1).
domain_ns_analytical_capability_absence_: The active scope's language has no NER / sentiment backbone, so this question is structurally unanswerable (WP-002 / WP-004).
domain_ns_k_anonymity_suppression_desc: Data exists but is withheld to protect k-anonymity — an ethical choice, distinct from publisher omission (WP-006 §7.2.2).
domain_ns_equivalence_refusal_desc: Cross-frame normalisation was requested without a granted metric equivalence — refused rather than coerced (WP-004 §5.3).
domain_ns_thin_content_desc: The article body is below the prose-length floor (a live-blog / photo / listing-page stub) — collected and disclosed, never silently dropped or counted as full prose (WP-007 §4.3).
domain_ns_live_ticker_desc: A live-ticker / continuously-updated page (a stable URL re-captured past the revision cap) — its endless versions are bounded and the article is excluded from the analytical reading, disclosed rather than silently dropped (WP-007 §4.3).
domain_ns_collection_completeness_desc: This source's corpus is N % complete for the period — the share of the publisher-declared in-window inventory that reached Gold, measured against its declared channels; the un-captured remainder (and any channel with no measurable denominator) is named, never assumed to be zero or 100 % (WP-007 §4.1, §7).
domain_ns_policy_overlay: Negative Space: when the toggle is on, the artefacts excluded as methodologically meaningless are ghost-rendered here (dim) — shown for disclosure, not re-admitted to the counts (WP-005 §3.1).
domain_ns_policy_gap: Negative Space: intervals dominated by unobserved articles break the line rather than read as zero — an observation gap is not a discourse gap (WP-005 §3.1).
domain_ns_policy_badge: Negative Space: per-article markers (∅) appear in the article list and the source dossier; this aggregate view never coerces an absent value to zero (WP-003 §3.2).
domain_ns_policy_refuse: Negative Space: an aggregate over a structurally-absent field is refused here rather than coerced to a zero (WP-003 §3.2).
domain_ns_policy_noop: Negative Space has no per-article rendering for this view — the NS-classes do not change its structure. See the article list (∅) and the source dossier (WP-006 §4.2).
domain_methnote_aleph_merged_ts_body: the time series reflects the joint corpus, not per-source framings. Interpret cross-source comparability cautiously, especially when sources span multiple cultural-linguistic frames.
domain_methnote_aleph_merged_dist_body: the histogram aggregates the joint corpus, not per-source distributions. Per-source comparability may be affected by source heterogeneity, especially across cultural-linguistic frames.
domain_methnote_episteme_jc_dist_body: topics reflect the joint corpus, not per-source framings. Source-specific framings may be aggregated away.
domain_methnote_episteme_jc_evo_body: streams aggregate the joint corpus, not per-source framings. Source-specific framings may be aggregated away.
domain_methnote_episteme_small_corpus_bo: BERTopic rarely converges on a coherent topic set below this threshold. Interpret topics cautiously.
domain_methnote_rhizome_leadlag_body: publication timing is measured on clock/calendar time — a culture-independent axis — so comparing the two probes’ rhythm is valid given verified DE/FR calendar parity. This is a when-comparison only; it asserts nothing about how much or how positive.
